interface: Key Vault REST API, api-version 2025-07-01 (date-based versions replaced the 7.x line)

list_secrets.maxresults: optional int32, minimum 1, maximum 25; service returns up to 25 when omitted

response.nextLink: absolute URL for the next page; OPAQUE -- follow it verbatim, never rebuild or re-version it (live service emits links whose api-version differs from the request, e.g. 7.2); absent/null when the listing is complete

secret_item.id: https://{vault}/secrets/{name} -- the name is the last path segment
secret_item.tags: object of string key/value pairs, optional
secret_item.managed: bool, true when the secret backs a certificate

list_secrets.note: only identifiers and attributes are returned; secret values are never included and the inventory must not fetch them

list_deleted_secrets.response: DeletedSecretListResult with the same value/nextLink paging shape
list_deleted_secrets.id_field: id still points at the original https://{vault}/secrets/{name} identifier

auth.header: Authorization: Bearer {token} on every request, including nextLink follows

distinct_statuses.401: authentication: missing/invalid/expired token (Key Vault answers with a WWW-Authenticate challenge)
distinct_statuses.403: authorization: valid token but missing permission, or the vault firewall blocked the caller
distinct_statuses.429: throttling: over the vault's transaction limits; clients must back off exponentially; Retry-After (seconds) may accompany it

errors.throttling_note: failed 429 responses do not count against the vault's throttle limits; recommended client backoff is 1s,2s,4s,8s,16s

test_fixture_notes.vault: the loopback mock stands in for https://ops-kv.vault.azure.net
